---
title: "flat_first.Rmd for working package"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

<!-- Run this 'development' chunk -->
<!-- Store every call to library() that you need to explore your functions -->

```{r development, include=FALSE}
library(testthat)
```

<!--
 You need to run the 'description' chunk in the '0-dev_history.Rmd' file before continuing your code there.

If it is the first time you use {fusen}, after 'description', you can directly run the last chunk of the present file with inflate() inside.
--> 

```{r development-load}
# Load already included functions if relevant
pkgload::load_all(export_all = FALSE)
```

# Include some data examples in your package

<!-- 
 Store your dataset in a directory named "inst/" at the root of your project.
 Use it for your tests in this Rmd thanks to `pkgload::load_all()` to make it available
and `system.file()` to read it in your examples.

- There already is a dataset in the "inst/" directory to be used in the examples below
-->

```{r development-dataset}
# Run all this chunk in the console directly
# There already is a dataset in the "inst/" directory
# Make the dataset file available to the current Rmd during development
pkgload::load_all(path = here::here(), export_all = FALSE)

# You will be able to read your example data file in each of your function examples and tests as follows - see chunks below
datafile <- system.file(c("pop2024.RDS", "pop2025.RDS","pop2026.RDS","antennes.RDS", "vacc_data.RDS", "shape.RDS"), package = "pevrdc")

pop2024 <- readRDS(datafile[1])
pop2025 <- readRDS(datafile[2])
pop2026 <- readRDS(datafile[3])
antennes <- readRDS(datafile[4])
vacc_data <- readRDS(datafile[5])
shape <- readRDS(datafile[6])

usethis::use_data(pop2024, overwrite = TRUE)
usethis::use_data(pop2025, overwrite = TRUE)
usethis::use_data(pop2026, overwrite = TRUE)
usethis::use_data(antennes, overwrite = TRUE)
usethis::use_data(vacc_data, overwrite = TRUE)
usethis::use_data(shape, overwrite = TRUE)
```


# Removing accents from character values stored in variables  of DHIS2 datasets

<!--
Create a chunk for the core of the function

- The chunk needs to be named `function` at least
- It contains the code of a documented function
- The chunk can also be named `function-my_median` to make it easily
findable in your Rmd
- Let the `@examples` part empty, and use the next `examples` chunk instead to present reproducible examples

After inflating the template

-  This function code will automatically be added in a new file in the "R/" directory
-->

```{r development-function}
# Prepare the code of your function here
```

```{r function-correct_accent}
#' A function to remove accented characters from all the character values of a data frame
#'
#' @param data Dataframe, usually the DHIS2 immunization dataframe
#' @param pattern A character value to match or a regular expression. Default "Ã©|Ã©"
#' @param replacement A character value to replace the pattern. Default "e"
#'
#' @return
#' A dataframe with values corrected
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
correct_accent <- function(data, pattern = "\u00C3\u00A9", replacement = "e") {
  variables <- colnames(data)
  data_list <- Map(function(x)data[[x]] <- gsub(pattern, replacement, data[[x]], ), variables)
  data <- as.data.frame(data_list)

  colnames(data) <- tolower(gsub("\\.", "_", colnames(data)))

  return(data)
}


```

<!--
Create a chunk with an example of use for your function

- The chunk needs to be named `examples` at least
- It contains working examples of your function
- The chunk is better be named `examples-my_median` to be handled
correctly when inflated as a vignette

After inflating the template

-  This example will automatically be added in the '@examples' part of our function above in the "R/" directory
- This example will automatically be added in the vignette created from this Rmd template
-->

```{r examples-correct_accent}
datafile <- system.file(c("vacc_data.RDS"), package = "pevrdc")

vacc <- readRDS(datafile)
correct_accent(vacc, pattern =  "\u00C3\u00A9", replacement = "e")
```

<!--
Create a chunk with a test of use for your function

- The chunk needs to be named `tests` at least
- It contains working tests of your function
- The chunk is better be named `tests-my_median` to be handled
correctly when inflated as a vignette

After inflating the template

-  This test code will automatically be added in the "tests/testthat/" directory
-->

```{r tests-correct_accent}
df <- data.frame(A = c("aè", "bè", "cè"), B = c("èt", "èx", "èy"))

df_correct <- correct_accent(df, "è", "e")

test_that("correct_accent replaces all the accented characters in the dataset", {
  expect_true(sum(grepl("e$", df_correct$a)) == sum(grepl("è$", df$A)) )
  expect_true(sum(grepl("^e", df_correct$b)) == sum(grepl("^è", df$B)) )
})


```


# Tally of vaccinated 


```{r function-vaccine_sums}
#' A function to compute the cummulative monthly tally of vaccinated per organization levels using DHIS2 exported data
#'
#' @param data Dataframe, usually the DHIS2 immunization dataframe
#' @param vaccine Column name of the vaccine of interest. Should be one of the following: BCG, DTC1, DTC3, VPO0, VAA, VAR1, VAR2, VPI1, VPI2, Pneumo13, Rota1, Rota2, Rota3, VAP1, VAP2, VAP3, VAP4, DTC2, Pneumo13_2, VPI 2, VPO1, VPO2, VPO3 & TD2 
#' @param level The column name or vector of column names of the aggregation level of interest. Should be any of Country, Province, District, or Antenne 
#' @param month An integer between 1 to 12 that specifies the cummulative period of months to aggregate date
#' @param accent A logical to specify whether the dataset values contains accented characters. Set to TRUE as default 
#' @importFrom stats aggregate reformulate setNames
#' @return
#' A dataframe with aggregated sums of vaccinated between 
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
vaccine_sums <- function(data, vaccine, level, month = 1, accent = TRUE) {
  vacc_list <- c(
    "BCG" = "bcg_0_et_11_mois",
    "DTC1" = "dtc_hepb_hib1_0_et_11",
    "DTC3" = "dtc_hepb_hib3_0_et_11",
    "VPO0" = "vpo_0_0_et_11",
    "VAA" = "vaa_0_et_11",
    "VAR1" = "var_1_rr_1_0_et_11",
    "VAR2" = "var_2_rr_2_0_et_11_mois|var_2_rr_2_12_et_23_mois",
    "VPI1" = "vpi_1_0_et_11",
    "VPI2" = "vpi_2_0_et_11",
    "Pneumo13" = "pcv_13__1__0_et_11",
    "Rota1" = "rota_1_0_et_11",
    "Rota2" = "rota_2_0_et_11",
    "Rota3" = "rota_3_0_et_11",
    "VAP1" = "vap_1_0_et_11",
    "VAP2" = "vap_2_0_et_11",
    "VAP3" = "vap_3_0_et_11",
    "VAP4" = "vap_4_0_et_11|vap_4_12_et_23",
    "DTC2" = "dtc_hepb_hib2_0_et_11",
    "Pneumo13_2" = "pcv_13__2__0_et_11",
    "VPI 2" = "vpi_2_0_et_11_mois",
    "VPO1" = "vpo_1_0_et_11",
    "VPO2" = "vpo_2_0_et_11",
    "VPO3" = "vpo_3_0_et_11",
    "TD2" = "td_[2-5]"
  )

  unit <- c(
    "Country" = "orgunitlevel1", "Province" = "orgunitlevel2", "District" = "orgunitlevel3", "Aire" = "orgunitlevel4",
    "Fosa" = "orgunitlevel5", "Antenne" = "orgunitlevel6"
  )

  vaccine1 <- {if(is.character(deparse1(substitute(vaccine)))) as.character(substitute(vaccine)) else deparse1(substitute(vaccine))}

  level1 <- eval(substitute(level), stats::setNames(as.list(unit), names(unit)), parent.frame())


  stopifnot(length(vaccine1) == 1, !is.null(data), vaccine1 %in% names(vacc_list), all(level1 %in% unit), !is.null(level1), !is.null(vaccine1))
  
  if(month < 1 || month > 12)
    stop("month should be between 1 and 12")
  
  if(accent)
    data <- correct_accent(data)

  level <- level1
  vacc_name <- vaccine1
  vaccine <- vacc_list[vaccine1]
  # Adding antenna
  if (any(grepl("Antenne", names(level)))) {
    # Adding antenna
    data <- add_antennes(data, dhis = TRUE)
    message("Antennes were added to the data")
  }


  data <- do.call(cbind, list(data["periodname"], data[level], Map(as.numeric, data[grepl(vaccine, colnames(data))])))


  # removing all NAs
  data[is.na(data)] <- 0
  # Calculating the period

  syst_time <- Sys.getlocale("LC_TIME")

  Sys.setlocale(category = "LC_TIME", locale = "English_United States.utf8")


  period <- c(
    "Janvier" = 1, "Fevrier" = 2, "Mars" = 3, "Avril" = 4, "Mai" = 5, "Juin" = 6, "Juillet" = 7,
    "Aout" = 8, "Septembre" = 9, "Octobre" = 10, "Novembre" = 11, "Decembre" = 12
  )
  period_eng <- c(
    "January" = 1, "February" = 2, "March" = 3, "April" = 4, "May" = 5, "June" = 6, "July" = 7,
    "August" = 8, "September" = 9, "October" = 10, "November" = 11, "December" = 12
  )
  if (all(nchar(as.character(data[["periodname"]])) == 10)) {
    data[, "periodname"] <- as.Date(data[["periodname"]])
    data$period <- period_eng[format(data$periodname, "%B")]
  } else {
    data$period <- unname(period[trimws(gsub("[0-9]", "", data$periodname), "both")])
  }
  
  data <- subset(data, period <= month)

  on.exit(Sys.setlocale(category = "LC_TIME", locale = syst_time))

  data$vaccsum <- Reduce(`+`, data[, grepl(vaccine, colnames(data))])

  vacc_agg <- stats::aggregate(stats::reformulate(level, response = "vaccsum"), data = data, FUN = sum)


  names(vacc_agg)[names(vacc_agg) == "vaccsum"] <- vacc_name

  return(vacc_agg)
}




```

<!--
Create a chunk with an example of use for your function

- The chunk needs to be named `examples` at least
- It contains working examples of your function
- The chunk is better be named `examples-my_median` to be handled
correctly when inflated as a vignette

After inflating the template

-  This example will automatically be added in the '@examples' part of our function above in the "R/" directory
- This example will automatically be added in the vignette created from this Rmd template
-->

```{r examples-vaccine_sums}
datafile <- system.file(c("vacc_data.RDS"), package = "pevrdc")

vacc <- readRDS(datafile)
vaccine_sums(vacc, DTC1, District, 2)
```

<!--
Create a chunk with a test of use for your function

- The chunk needs to be named `tests` at least
- It contains working tests of your function
- The chunk is better be named `tests-my_median` to be handled
correctly when inflated as a vignette

After inflating the template

-  This test code will automatically be added in the "tests/testthat/" directory
-->

```{r tests-vaccine_sums}
datafile <- system.file(c("vacc_data.RDS"), package = "pevrdc")

vacc <- readRDS(datafile)
penta1 <- vaccine_sums(vacc, DTC1, District, 2)
penta3 <- vaccine_sums(vacc, DTC3, District, 2)

# Apply test on my function
test_that("vaccine_sums coherently aggregates vaccines numbers", {
  expect_true(sum(penta3$DTC3) <= sum(penta1$DTC1))
})
```


# Calculate target populations

<!--
There can be other functions, examples and tests in your flat template.
Each of them will be inflated in a different file, provided that there is a level-1 or level-2 section title to separate from previous functions.
-->

## Use sub-functions in the same chunk

```{r function-agg_pop}
#' A function that aggregates the immunization population tallies of DRC per organization level using built-in 2025 population dataset
#'
#' @param var Column name of the variable containing the population numbers. For the pops dataset monthly targets immunization use naissance_mois_ajust or survivant_mois_ajust. For surveillance use population_totale  
#' @param group The organization level orgunitlevel1 for country, orgunitlevel2 for province, orgunitlevel3 for district and orgunitlevel6 for antennes
#' @param month A numeric value between 1 and 12 to specify the number of months for immunization targets
#' @param pops A dataframe with population data 
#'
#' @return
#' A dataframe of aggregated populations 
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
agg_pop <- function(var, group, month = 1, pops) {

  agg_pop_q(substitute(var), substitute(group), month, pops = pops)
}


#' A version of agg_pop that does not use NSE, more convenient to use within other functions. Variables arguments are characters
#'
#' @param var Column name of the variable containing the population numbers. For the pops dataset monthly targets immunization use naissance_mois_ajust or survivant_mois_ajust. For surveillance use population_totale  
#' @param group The organization level orgunitlevel1 for country, orgunitlevel2 for province, orgunitlevel3 for district and orgunitlevel6 for antennes
#' @param month A numeric value between 1 and 12 to specify the number of months for immunization targets
#' @param pops A dataframe with population data
#' @importFrom stats aggregate reformulate
#' @return
#' A dataframe of aggregated populations 
#' @export
#'

agg_pop_2 <- function(var, group, month = 1, pops) {
  # --- Argument checks ---
  stopifnot(is.character(var), length(var) == 1)
  stopifnot(is.character(group), length(group) >= 1)
  stopifnot(is.numeric(month), month >= 1, month <= 12)
  stopifnot(var %in% names(pops))
  stopifnot(all(group %in% names(pops)))

  # --- Aggregate ---
  agg_data <- stats::aggregate(
    stats::reformulate(group, response = var),
    data = pops,
    FUN = sum
  )

  # --- Apply month multiplier when appropriate ---
  if (!grepl("totale", var))
    agg_data[[var]] <- agg_data[[var]] * month

  return(agg_data)
}


#' Core of agg_pop not exported. A function that aggregates the immunization population tallies of DRC per organization level using built-in 2025 population dataset
#' @param var Column name of the variable containing the population numbers. For the pops dataset monthly targets immunization use naissance_mois_ajust or survivant_mois_ajust. For surveillance use population_totale  
#' @param group The organization level orgunitlevel1 for country, orgunitlevel2 for province, orgunitlevel3 for district and orgunitlevel6 for antennes
#' @param month A numeric value between 1 and 12 to specify the number of months for immunization targets
#' @param pops  A dataframe with population data
#' @importFrom stats aggregate reformulate setNames
#' @return
#' A dataframe of aggregated populations 
agg_pop_q <- function(var, group, month = 1, pops) {

  var1 <- {if(is.character(var)) as.character(var) else eval(var, stats::setNames(as.list(names(pops)), names(pops)), parent.frame())}
  group1 <- eval(group, stats::setNames(as.list(names(pops)), names(pops)), parent.frame())
  
   if(month < 1 || month > 12)
    stop("month should be between 1 and 12")
  
  
  stopifnot(is.numeric(pops[[var1]]))

  agg_data <- stats::aggregate(
    stats::reformulate(group1, response = var1),
    data = pops,
    FUN = sum
  )

  if(!grepl("totale",var1))
    agg_data[[var1]] <-agg_data[[var1]] * month

  return(agg_data)
}
```


```{r examples-agg_pop}

datafile <- system.file(c("pop2025.RDS"), package = "pevrdc")

pops <- readRDS(datafile)
agg_pop(naissance_mois_admin, orgunitlevel2, month = 8, pops = pops)

agg_pop_2("naissance_mois_admin", "orgunitlevel2", month = 8, pops = pops)
```

```{r tests-agg_pop}
datafile <- system.file(c("pop2025.RDS"), package = "pevrdc")

pops <- readRDS(datafile)
population <- agg_pop(naissance_mois_admin, orgunitlevel2, month = 1, pops = pops)

# Apply test on my function
test_that("agg_pop outputs a dataframe of 2 columns, one character and the other numeric", {
  expect_true(ncol(population) == 2)
  expect_false(is.numeric(population[,1]))
  expect_false(is.character(population[,2]))
})
```


# Vaccine Coverage 




```{r function-vaccine_coverage}
#' A function to compute the cummulative monthly vaccine coverage  per organization levels using DHIS2 exported data
#'
#' @param data Dataframe, usually the DHIS2 immunization dataframe already aggregated
#' @param vaccine Column name of the vaccine of interest. Should be one of the following: BCG, DTC1, DTC3, VPO0, VAA, VAR1, VAR2, VPI1, VPI2, Pneumo13, Rota1, Rota2, Rota3, VAP1, VAP2, VAP3, VAP4, DTC2, Pneumo13_2, VPI 2, VPO1, VPO2, VPO3 & TD2 
#' @param level The column name or vector of column names of the aggregation level of interest. Should be any of Country, Province, District or Antenne 
#' @param month An integer between 1 to 12 that specifies the cummulative period of months to aggregate date
#' @param pops An aggregated dataset of target populations.
#' @importFrom stats aggregate reformulate setNames
#' @return
#' A dataframe with vaccines coverage 
#' @export
#'
#' @examples

vaccine_coverage <- function(data, vaccine, level, month = 1, pops) {
  vac <- c(
    "BCG", "DTC1", "DTC3", "VPO0", "VAA", "VAR1", "VAR2", "VPI1",
    "VPI2", "Pneumo13", "Rota1", "Rota2", "Rota3", "VAP1", "VAP2",
    "VAP3", "VAP4", "DTC2", "Pneumo13_2", "VPI 2", "VPO1", "VPO2",
    "VPO3", "TD2"
  )
  unit <- c(
    "Country" = "orgunitlevel1", "Province" = "orgunitlevel2", "District" = "orgunitlevel3", "Aire" = "orgunitlevel4",
    "Fosa" = "orgunitlevel5", "Antenne" = "orgunitlevel6"
  )

  level1 <- eval(substitute(level), stats::setNames(as.list(unit), names(unit)), parent.frame())

  vaccine1 <-{if(is.character(deparse1(substitute(vaccine)))) as.character(substitute(vaccine)) else deparse1(substitute(vaccine))}

  vaccine <- vaccine1

  stopifnot(is.numeric(month), !is.null(data), vaccine %in% vac, vaccine %in% colnames(data), all(level1 %in% unit))

  level <- level1



  data <- stats::aggregate(stats::reformulate(level, response = vaccine), FUN = sum, data = data)


  if (vaccine %in% c("BCG", "VPO0")) {
    num_vars <- c("naissance_mois_admin", "naissance_mois_ajust")
  } else {
    num_vars <- c("survivants_mois_admin", "survivant_mois_ajust")
  }

  pop1 <- agg_pop_2(num_vars[1],level, month = month, pops = pops)
  pop2 <- agg_pop_2(num_vars[2],level, month = month, pops = pops)


  populations <- merge(pop1, pop2, by = level)



  merged_data <- merge(data, populations, by = level)



  merged_data[[paste0(vaccine, "_admin")]] <- round(100 * merged_data[[vaccine]] / merged_data[[num_vars[1]]], 2)
  merged_data[[paste0(vaccine, "_ajust")]] <- round(100 * merged_data[[vaccine]] / merged_data[[num_vars[2]]], 2)

  if(vaccine == "DTC1"){
    merged_data[["zero_dose_admin"]] <- round(merged_data[[num_vars[1]]] - merged_data[[vaccine]])
    merged_data[["zero_dose_ajust"]] <- round(merged_data[[num_vars[2]]] - merged_data[[vaccine]])

  }

  return(merged_data)
}


```

<!--
Create a chunk with an example of use for your function

- The chunk needs to be named `examples` at least
- It contains working examples of your function
- The chunk is better be named `examples-my_median` to be handled
correctly when inflated as a vignette

After inflating the template

-  This example will automatically be added in the '@examples' part of our function above in the "R/" directory
- This example will automatically be added in the vignette created from this Rmd template
-->

```{r examples-vaccine_coverage}
datafile <- system.file(c("vacc_data.RDS", "pop2025.RDS"), package = "pevrdc")

vacc <- readRDS(datafile[1])
pops <- readRDS(datafile[2])
vacc_tots <- vaccine_sums(vacc, DTC1, District, 2)
vaccine_coverage(vacc_tots, DTC1, District, month = 2, pops = pops)

```

<!--
Create a chunk with a test of use for your function

- The chunk needs to be named `tests` at least
- It contains working tests of your function
- The chunk is better be named `tests-my_median` to be handled
correctly when inflated as a vignette

After inflating the template

-  This test code will automatically be added in the "tests/testthat/" directory
-->

```{r tests-vaccine_coverage}

datafile <- system.file(c("vacc_data.RDS", "pop2025.RDS"), package = "pevrdc")

vacc <- readRDS(datafile[1])
pops <- readRDS(datafile[2])
penta1 <- vaccine_sums(vacc, DTC1, District, 2)
penta1_cov <- vaccine_coverage(penta1, DTC1, District, month = 2, pops = pops)
penta3 <- vaccine_sums(vacc, DTC3, District, 2)
penta3_cov <- vaccine_coverage(penta3, DTC3, District, month = 2, pops = pops)
variables1 <- c("orgunitlevel3", "DTC1", "survivants_mois_admin", "survivant_mois_ajust", 
"DTC1_admin", "DTC1_ajust", "zero_dose_admin", "zero_dose_ajust"
)
variables2 <- c("orgunitlevel3", "DTC3", "survivants_mois_admin", "survivant_mois_ajust", 
"DTC3_admin", "DTC3_ajust"
)






test_that("vaccine_coverage outputs expected variables and variables types", {
  expect_true(all(colnames(penta1_cov) == variables1))
  expect_true(all(colnames(penta3_cov) == variables2))
  types <- table(unlist(Map(function(x)typeof(penta1_cov[[x]]), variables1)))
  expect_true(types["double"] == 7)
  types2 <- table(unlist(Map(function(x)typeof(penta3_cov[[x]]), variables2)))
  expect_true(types2["double"] == 5)

 # expect_error(my_median("text"))
})


```



# Cleaning names 


<!--
There can be other functions, examples and tests in your flat template.
Each of them will be inflated in a different file, provided that there is a level-1 or level-2 section title to separate from previous functions.
-->

## Use sub-functions in the same chunk

```{r function-clean_dhis_names}
#' A function that removes DRC DHIS prefixes and suffixes 
#'
#' @param data The dataset containing variables to be cleaned  
#' @param column The column containing values to be cleaned espcially organization levels of DRC EPI
#' @param has_prefix An integer specifying the number of characters of the prefix to be removed. Set to 0 if no prefix
#' @param suffix A character value that specifies the suffix to be removed
#'
#' @return
#' A dataframe with cleaned names
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
clean_dhis_names <- function(data, column, has_prefix = 2, 
                             suffix = "(Province|Zone de Sant[e\u00e9]|Aire de Sant[e\u00e9]|Centre de Sant[e\u00e9])"){
 clean_dhis_names_q(data, substitute(column), has_prefix, suffix)

}



#' Core of clean_dhis_names not exported.#'
#' @param data The dataset containing variables to be cleaned  
#' @param column The column containing values to be cleaned espcially organization levels of DRC EPI
#' @param has_prefix An integer specifying the number of characters of the prefix to be removed. Set to 0 if no prefix
#' @param suffix A character value that specifies the suffix to be removed
#'
#' @return
#' A dataframe with cleaned names

clean_dhis_names_q <- function(data, column, has_prefix = 2,
                               suffix = "(Province|Zone de Sant[e\u00e9]|Aire de Sant[e\u00e9]|Centre de Sant[e\u00e9])"){

  column1 <- eval(column, setNames(as.list(names(data)), names(data)), parent.frame())

  column <- column1

  stopifnot(!is.null(data), !is.null(column), is.character(column), is.character(suffix), is.numeric(has_prefix), length(column) == 1)

  if (!column %in% names(data)) stop("Column not found in data: ", column)

  has_prefix <- as.integer(has_prefix)

  suffix <- gsub("([.|()\\^{}+$*?]|\\[|\\]|\\\\)", "\\\\\\1", suffix)

  string <- paste0("(^[A-Za-z]", "{", has_prefix,"})|(", suffix,"$)")

  data[[column]] <- trimws(gsub(string, "", data[[column]], perl = TRUE), "both")

  if(suffix == "") warning("No suffix was trimmed")

  if(has_prefix == 0) warning("No prefix was trimmed")

  return(data)


}



#' A function that removes DRC's DHIS 2 prefixes and suffixes  from a vector
#'
#' @param x A character vector to be cleaned especially organization levels of DRC EPI. More convenient to use within other functions
#' @param has_prefix An integer specifying the number of characters of the prefix to be removed. Set to 0 if no prefix
#' @param suffix A character value that specifies the suffix to be removed
#'
#' @return
#' A character vector
#' @export
#'



clean_names_dhis <- function(x, has_prefix = 2, 
                             suffix = "(Province|Zone de Sant[e\u00e9]|Aire de Sant[e\u00e9]|Centre de Sant[e\u00e9])") {
  stopifnot(is.character(x), !is.null(x),  is.character(suffix), is.numeric(has_prefix))
  pattern <- paste0("(^[A-Za-z]{", has_prefix, "}\\s*)|(\\s*", suffix, "$)")

  cleaned <- trimws(gsub(pattern, "", x, perl = TRUE))

  return(cleaned)
}





```

```{r examples-clean_dhis_names}

datafile <- system.file(c("pop2025.RDS"), package = "pevrdc")

vacc <- readRDS(datafile)
clean_dhis_names(vacc, column = orgunitlevel2, has_prefix = 2, suffix = "Province")

clean_names_dhis(vacc$orgunitlevel2,  has_prefix = 2,
                 suffix = "(Province|Zone de Sant[e\u00e9]|Aire de Sant[e\u00e9]|Centre de Sant[e\u00e9])")
```

```{r tests-clean_dhis_names}
datafile <- system.file(c("pop2025.RDS"), package = "pevrdc")

pop <- readRDS(datafile)

pop <- clean_dhis_names(pop, column = orgunitlevel3, has_prefix = 2, suffix = "Zone de Sante")




test_that("clean_dhis_names removes all the suffixes", {
  expect_true(sum(grepl("(Zone de Sante)$", pop$orgunitlevel1)) == 0)
})
```

# Antenne


```{r function-add_antennes}
#' A function that adds DRC antennes to a dataset not having them
#'
#' @param data A dataset with no variable containing antennes, usually DHIS 2 datasets  
#' @param dhis A boolean to indicate whether the dataset is from the DHIS 2, set to TRUE as default
#' @param zone_sante A variable with health zones names if data are not from DHIS 2, set to NULL as default
#' @param rename_antenne A logical to indicate renaming antennes following DHIS 2 standard 
#'
#' @return
#' A dataframe with a variable with antennes
#' @export
#'

add_antennes <- function(data, dhis = TRUE, zone_sante = NULL, rename_antenne = TRUE) {
  stopifnot(!is.null(data), is.data.frame(data))
  
  if (!dhis) {
    if (!dhis & missing(zone_sante)) {
      stop("zone_sante variable not supplied as data are not from the DHIS 2")
    }
  }
  
  antennes <- get("antennes", "package:hpdDRC")
  
  if (!dhis && !missing(zone_sante)) zone_sante <- deparse1(substitute(zone_sante))
  
  if (!dhis) {
    merged_data <- tryCatch({merge(data, antennes, by.x = zone_sante, by.y = "zonesante", rename_antenne = TRUE)}, 
                            error = function(e) message("There is an error: ",e))
    
    return(merged_data)
  }
  
  data$zs <- trimws(gsub("^[A-Za-z]{2}|Zone de Sant[e\u00e9]$", "", data[["orgunitlevel3"]]), "both")
  
  merged_data <- merge(data, antennes, by.x = "zs", by.y = "zonesante")
  
  merged_data$zs <- NULL
  
  if (rename_antenne) {
    merged_data$orgunitlevel6 <- merged_data$Antenne
    
    merged_data$Antenne <- NULL
    
    cat("Antenne column is renamed orgunitlevel6")
  }
  
  
  return(merged_data)
}


```



# Outbreak classification





```{r function-is_outbreak}
#' This is function returns TRUE after n successive TRUE and FALSE otherwise. This is a convenient function to classify areas with outbreak following consecutive
#' positive cases of diseases recorded within n weeks.
#' @param x an ordered logical vector 
#' @param nwk a numeric value that specifies the window frame for the assessment of consecutive logical values of x 
#' @return
#' A logical vecor of similar length with x
#' @export
#'
#' @examples

is_outbreak <- function(x, nwk = 4) {
  stopifnot(is.logical(x)|is.numeric(x))
  if(is.numeric(x)){
    x <- as.logical(x)
    warning("x was coerced to logical")
  }
  if (anyNA(x)){
    warning("NA values present; treated as FALSE")
    x[is.na(x)] <- FALSE}

  sapply(seq_along(x), function(i) {
    sum(x[max((i - nwk + 1),0):i]) >= 3
  })
}



```

<!--
Create a chunk with an example of use for your function

- The chunk needs to be named `examples` at least
- It contains working examples of your function
- The chunk is better be named `examples-my_median` to be handled
correctly when inflated as a vignette

After inflating the template

-  This example will automatically be added in the '@examples' part of our function above in the "R/" directory
- This example will automatically be added in the vignette created from this Rmd template
-->

```{r examples-is_outbreak}

is_outbreak(c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE), 4)

```

<!--
Create a chunk with a test of use for your function

- The chunk needs to be named `tests` at least
- It contains working tests of your function
- The chunk is better be named `tests-my_median` to be handled
correctly when inflated as a vignette

After inflating the template

-  This test code will automatically be added in the "tests/testthat/" directory
-->

```{r tests-is_outbreak}

x <- c(FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE)

test_that("is_outbreak  works properly and show error if needed", {
  expect_equal(sum(is_outbreak(x)), 7)
})

```




```{r function-district_epi}
#' This is function is wrapper around is_outbreak to identify districts with an outbreak. 
#' @param df A dataset with a weekly number of cases or a logical indication of a case detected
#' @param district A variable containing districts names
#' @param wk A numeric variable specifying the successive week numbers 
#' @param status A logical or numeric variable with either indication of case recorded or the number of cases
#' @param nwk A numeric value that specifies the window frame for the assessment of consecutive logical values of x 
#' @param sorted A logical value to specify whether the dataset is ordered per week and district
#' @param pre_yr A logical value to specify whether dataset contains data from the previous year
#' @param year A numeric variable containing the year of the data
#' @return
#' A dataframe with an additional logical variable epi_status
#' @export
#'
#' @examples
district_epi <- function(df, district, wk, status, nwk = 4, sorted = FALSE, pre_yr = FALSE, year = NULL){

  district <- deparse1(substitute(district))

  wk <- deparse1(substitute(wk))

  status <- deparse1(substitute(status))

  if(!is.null(year))
    year <- deparse1(substitute(year))

  stopifnot(!is.null(df), !is.null(district), !is.null(wk), !is.null(status))


  if(!sorted)
    df <- df[order(df[[district]], df[[wk]]),]

  if(pre_yr){
    if(is.null(year))
      stop("year must be specified if pre_yr is set to TRUE")
    yr <- sort(unique(as.numeric(df[[year]])))
    if (length(yr) != 2)
      stop("pre_yr = TRUE requires exactly two distinct years in the dataset.")
    if((yr[2] - 1)!= yr[1])
      stop("Years are not consecutive")

    pre <- df[df[[year]] == yr[1]]

    current <- df[df[[year]] == yr[2]]

    pre <- pre[order(pre[[district]], pre[[wk]]),]

    current <- current[order(current[[district]], current[[wk]]),]

    df <- rbind(pre, current)}



  sp <- split(df, df[[district]])


  for (i in 1:length(sp)){

    sp[[i]][["epi_status"]] <- is_outbreak(sp[[i]][[status]], nwk = nwk)

  }

  epi_districts <- do.call(rbind, sp)

  row.names(epi_districts) <- NULL

  return(epi_districts)


}


```

<!--
Create a chunk with an example of use for your function

- The chunk needs to be named `examples` at least
- It contains working examples of your function
- The chunk is better be named `examples-my_median` to be handled
correctly when inflated as a vignette

After inflating the template

-  This example will automatically be added in the '@examples' part of our function above in the "R/" directory
- This example will automatically be added in the vignette created from this Rmd template
-->

```{r examples-district_epi}

df <- data.frame(district = c(rep("A", 4), rep("B", 5)),
                 week = c(1:4, 1:5), 
                 status = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE))

district_epi(df, district, week, status, 3, sorted = TRUE)

```



# Data 


```{r function-pop2024}
#' A dataset containing DRC's 2024 target immunization populations at health districts levels
#'
#' @format A data frame with 519 observations and 6 variables:
#' \describe{
#'\item{orgunitlevel2}{character; Province}
#'\item{orgunitlevel3}{character; Zone de santé (health district)  }
#'\item{naissance_mois_admin}{numeric; monthly number of livebirths}
#'\item{survivants_mois_admin}{numeric; monthly number of surviving children}
#'\item{naissance_mois_ajust}{numeric; monthly number of livebirths ajusted}
#'\item{survivant_mois_ajust}{numeric; monthly number of surviving children ajusted}
#'\item{orgunitlevel6}{character; antenne}
#' }
#' @source Programme Élargie de Vaccination de RDC.
"pop2024"

```



```{r function-pop2025}
#' A dataset containing DRC's 2025 target immunization populations at health districts levels
#'
#' @format A data frame with 519 observations and 6 variables:
#' \describe{
#'\item{orgunitlevel2}{character; Province}
#'\item{orgunitlevel3}{character; Zone de santé (health district)  }
#'\item{naissance_mois_admin}{numeric; monthly number of livebirths}
#'\item{survivants_mois_admin}{numeric; monthly number of surviving children}
#'\item{naissance_mois_ajust}{numeric; monthly number of livebirths ajusted}
#'\item{survivant_mois_ajust}{numeric; monthly number of surviving children ajusted}
#'\item{orgunitlevel6}{character; antenne}
#' }
#' @source Programme Élargie de Vaccination de RDC.
"pop2025"

```



```{r function-pop2026}
#' A dataset containing DRC's 2024 target immunization populations at health districts levels
#'
#' @format A data frame with 519 observations and 6 variables:
#' \describe{
#'\item{orgunitlevel2}{character; Province}
#'\item{orgunitlevel3}{character; Zone de santé (health district)  }
#'\item{naissance_mois_admin}{numeric; monthly number of livebirths}
#'\item{survivants_mois_admin}{numeric; monthly number of surviving children}
#'\item{naissance_mois_ajust}{numeric; monthly number of livebirths ajusted}
#'\item{survivant_mois_ajust}{numeric; monthly number of surviving children ajusted}
#'\item{orgunitlevel6}{character; antenne}
#' }
#' @source Programme Élargie de Vaccination de RDC.
"pop2026"

```




```{r function-antennes}
#' A dataset containing DRC's health antennas and health districts
#'
#' @format A data frame with 519 observations and 3 variables:
#' \describe{
#'\item{province}{character; provinces}
#'\item{antenne}{character; health antennas}
#'\item{zonesante}{character; health zones}
#' }
#' @source Programme Élargie de Vaccination de RDC.
"antennes"
```


```{r function-vacc_data}
#' A dataset with simulated DHIS 2 imuundata containing DRC's health antennas and health districts
#' Please refer to DHIS 2 for a complete codebook of the data
#' @format A data frame with 1000 observations and 225 variables:
#' @source Programme Élargie de Vaccination de RDC.
"vacc_data"
```


```{r function-shape}
#' A dataset with the DRC shapefile at health zone level, necessitate the sf package to load.
#' @format A data frame with 519 observations and 5 variables:
#' \describe{
#'\item{pays}{character}
#'\item{province}{character}
#'\item{antenne}{character}
#'\item{zonesante}{character}
#'\item{SHAPE}{multipolygon}
#' }
#' @source GRID-3.
"shape"
```







<!--
# There can be development actions

Create a chunk with 'development' actions

- The chunk needs to be named `development` or `dev`
- It contains functions that are used for package development only
- Note that you may want to store most of these functions in the 0-dev_history.Rmd file

These are only included in the present flat template file, their content will not be part of the package anywhere else.
-->

```{r development-inflate, eval=FALSE}
# Keep eval=FALSE to avoid infinite loop in case you hit the knit button
# Execute in the console directly
fusen::inflate(flat_file = "dev/flat_first.Rmd", vignette_name = "Get started")
```


# Inflate your package

You're one inflate from paper to box.
Build your package from this very Rmd using `fusen::inflate()`

- Verify your `"DESCRIPTION"` file has been updated
- Verify your function is in `"R/"` directory
- Verify your test is in `"tests/testthat/"` directory
- Verify this Rmd appears in `"vignettes/"` directory
